title: SummonsDispute
description: A respondent's request to contest an ECB summons — dispute it, request a hearing, submit a defense, or ask to adjourn/reschedule. NET-NEW: there is NO public API for responding to a summons today; a respondent (or their agent) can only do this through the legacy Apache Struts ECB Ticket Finder / Respond-to-a-Summons portal (a820-ecbticketfinder.nyc.gov), by mail, or in person. This schema serves both the write surface (submit a dispute) and the read surface (its status). Contesting a summons is the single most common OATH respondent transaction.

disputeId.description: Server-assigned dispute / request confirmation number. Omit on create; returned on the created resource.

ticketNumber.description: The ECB summons ('Ticket Number') being disputed.

requestType.description: What the respondent is asking for.

hearingChannel.description: How the respondent wishes to be heard.

defense.description: Free-text statement of defense / grounds for contesting the charges.

requestedHearingDate.description: Preferred hearing/adjournment date (subject to OATH availability).

adjournmentReason.description: Reason for an adjournment or reschedule request.

representative.description: Attorney or authorized agent responding on the respondent's behalf.

status.description: Lifecycle status. Set by OATH, not the requester.
